name: Barbell Bench Press
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with feet flat on floor
Grip bar slightly wider than shoulder width
Lower bar to mid-chest with control
Press back up to full extension
Keep back flat, do not arch excessively

name: Incline Dumbbell Press
instructions: Set bench to 30-45 degree incline
Hold dumbbells at shoulder level
Press up and slightly together
Lower with control to starting position

name: Push-Up
instructions: Place hands shoulder-width apart
Keep body straight from head to heels
Lower chest to floor
Push back to starting position
Keep core braced throughout

name: Cable Fly
instructions: Set cables at shoulder height
Stand in middle, one foot forward
Bring handles together in arc motion
Squeeze chest at peak contraction
Return slowly with control

name: Chest Dip
instructions: Grip parallel bars, arms straight
Lean forward slightly for chest focus
Lower until upper arms parallel to floor
Push back up to starting position

name: Decline Barbell Bench Press
instructions: Set bench to -15 to -30 degree decline
Hook feet under pads
Lower bar to lower chest
Press back up explosively
Use spotter for heavy weights

name: Dumbbell Fly
instructions: Lie flat on bench, dumbbells above chest
Lower arms in wide arc, slight elbow bend
Feel stretch in chest at bottom
Bring dumbbells back together in arc
Keep movement smooth and controlled

name: Pull-Up
instructions: Hang from bar with overhand grip
Pull yourself up until chin clears bar
Lower with full control to dead hang
Keep core engaged throughout
Avoid swinging or kipping

name: Barbell Deadlift
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width, bar over mid-foot
Hinge at hips, grip bar just outside legs
Keep chest up, back flat
Drive through floor to stand tall
Lower bar back under control

name: Lat Pulldown
instructions: Sit with thighs under pad, grip bar wide
Pull bar to upper chest, lean back slightly
Squeeze lats at bottom position
Return bar slowly to full arm extension
Keep shoulders depressed throughout

name: Seated Cable Row
instructions: Sit on cable row machine, feet on platform
Grip handle with both hands
Pull to lower abdomen, elbows close to sides
Squeeze shoulder blades together
Return with control, slight forward lean

name: Bent-Over Barbell Row
instructions: Hinge forward ~45°, bar hanging at arms length
Pull bar to lower chest or upper abdomen
Drive elbows back and squeeze back
Lower with control
Maintain neutral spine throughout

name: Single-Arm Dumbbell Row
instructions: Place one knee and hand on bench
Row dumbbell to hip, elbow close to body
Squeeze at top, lower slowly
Keep back parallel to floor
Complete all reps before switching sides

name: Face Pull
instructions: Set cable to head height, use rope attachment
Pull rope to face, elbows high and wide
Externally rotate at end of movement
Hold briefly, return with control
Great for shoulder health and posture

name: Barbell Back Squat
instructions: Bar across upper back, feet shoulder-width
Brace core, chest up
Squat until thighs parallel to floor
Drive through heels to stand
Keep knees tracking over toes

name: Leg Press
instructions: Sit in machine, feet shoulder-width on platform
Lower weight until knees at 90°
Push through heels to extend legs
Do not lock out knees at top
Keep lower back against pad

name: Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Stand with bar at hips, slight knee bend
Hinge at hips, pushing them back
Lower bar along legs until hamstring stretch
Drive hips forward to return to top
Keep back flat throughout movement

name: Walking Lunge
instructions: Stand tall, step forward into lunge
Lower back knee toward floor
Front knee stays over ankle
Push off front foot, bring feet together
Continue alternating legs forward

name: Lying Leg Curl
instructions: Lie face down on machine, pad behind ankles
Curl legs toward glutes
Hold briefly at peak contraction
Lower with control
Keep hips pressed into pad

name: Leg Extension
instructions: Sit in machine, pad on front of ankles
Extend legs to full straightness
Hold briefly at top
Lower with control
Keep back against seat pad

name: Standing Calf Raise
instructions: Stand on edge of platform, heels hanging
Rise up on toes as high as possible
Hold at top for 1-2 seconds
Lower heels below platform level
Full range of motion is key

name: Goblet Squat
instructions: Hold dumbbell vertically at chest
Feet shoulder-width, toes slightly out
Squat deep, elbows inside knees
Stand back up, driving through heels
Great for beginners learning squat pattern

name: Barbell Overhead Press
instructions: Grip bar just outside shoulders
Press from upper chest to full overhead extension
Lock out elbows at top
Lower back to clavicle with control
Brace core, do not lean back excessively

name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
instructions: Stand with dumbbells at sides
Raise arms out to sides to shoulder height
Slight bend in elbows throughout
Lower slowly — this is where growth happens
Avoid shrugging or using momentum

name: Dumbbell Front Raise
instructions: Stand with dumbbells at thighs
Raise one or both arms forward to shoulder height
Keep slight elbow bend
Lower with control
Avoid swinging torso

name: Reverse Dumbbell Fly
instructions: Hinge forward at hips ~45°
Raise arms out to sides, elbows slightly bent
Squeeze rear delts at top
Lower slowly to starting position
Keep back flat, not rounded

name: Arnold Press
instructions: Start with palms facing you at shoulder height
As you press up, rotate palms outward
Finish with palms facing forward at top
Reverse rotation as you lower
Full rotational range of motion

name: Barbell Curl
instructions: Stand with barbell at hip height, underhand grip
Curl bar to shoulder level, elbows fixed
Squeeze biceps at top
Lower slowly — 3 second negative
Keep upper arms still throughout

name: Dumbbell Curl
instructions: Stand or sit with dumbbells at sides
Curl one or both arms to shoulder
Supinate wrist at top for full contraction
Lower with control
Alternate arms or do simultaneously

name: Hammer Curl
instructions: Hold dumbbells in neutral grip (thumbs up)
Curl to shoulder keeping thumbs pointing up
Lower with control
Targets brachialis for thicker upper arm
Can be done alternating or together

name: Preacher Curl
instructions: Sit at preacher bench, arm over pad
Curl bar to chin with full range
Lower slowly to full extension
Preacher pad eliminates cheating
Great for peak bicep development

name: Tricep Pushdown
instructions: Set cable to high position, use bar or rope
Elbows at sides, push down to full extension
Squeeze triceps at bottom
Return slowly, elbows stay at sides
Rope version allows wrist rotation

name: Skull Crusher
instructions: Lie on bench, bar above chest
Lower bar toward forehead, elbows fixed
Extend arms back to starting position
Keep upper arms vertical and still
Use EZ bar to reduce wrist strain

name: Tricep Dip
instructions: Sit on bench, hands beside hips
Slide off bench, legs extended
Lower until elbows at 90°
Push back up to starting position
Keep torso upright for tricep focus

name: Overhead Tricep Extension
instructions: Hold dumbbell overhead with both hands
Lower behind head, elbows pointing up
Extend arms back to starting position
Keep elbows close to head
Full stretch of long head of tricep

name: Plank
instructions: Forearms on ground, elbows under shoulders
Body straight from head to heels
Brace core — imagine bracing for a punch
Hold position, breathe normally
Do not let hips sag or raise

name: Crunch
instructions: Lie on back, knees bent, hands at temples
Curl shoulders toward knees
Do not pull on neck
Lower with control
Exhale on the way up

name: Hanging Leg Raise
instructions: Hang from pull-up bar, arms straight
Raise legs to 90° or higher
Lower with control — no swinging
Keep shoulders packed
Posterior pelvic tilt at top for full contraction

name: Russian Twist
instructions: Sit with knees bent, lean back slightly
Hold weight or clasp hands together
Rotate torso side to side
Feet can be raised for more difficulty
Control the rotation — do not rush

name: Ab Wheel Rollout
instructions: Kneel on floor, hold ab wheel handles
Roll forward until body nearly parallel to floor
Pull back to starting position using core
Keep back flat throughout
Start with partial range if needed

name: Treadmill Run
instructions: Set speed appropriate to fitness level
Land mid-foot, not heel
Keep upright posture, slight forward lean
Arms at 90°, drive back not across
Use 1% incline to simulate outdoor running

name: Stationary Bike
instructions: Adjust seat so knee is slightly bent at bottom
Aim for 80-100 RPM cadence
Keep resistance challenging but sustainable
Engage core, avoid rocking hips
Great low-impact cardio option

name: Rowing Machine
instructions: Catch: knees bent, arms straight, body forward
Drive through legs first
As legs straighten, lean back slightly
Pull handle to lower chest
Return in reverse order: arms, body, legs

name: Elliptical Trainer
instructions: Keep weight in heels not toes
Use arm handles for full body workout
Maintain upright posture
Vary resistance and incline
Low impact — good for joint issues

name: Jump Rope
instructions: Hold handles at hip height
Jump just high enough to clear rope (2-3cm)
Land on balls of feet
Keep elbows in, wrists rotate the rope
Start with 30s intervals, build up

name: Barbell Hip Thrust
instructions: Sit against bench, bar across hips
Plant feet flat, shoulder-width apart
Drive hips up to form straight line shoulder-knee
Squeeze glutes hard at top
Lower with control

name: Glute Bridge
instructions: Lie on back, knees bent, feet flat
Drive hips up, squeezing glutes
Hold at top for 2 seconds
Lower slowly
Can add resistance with barbell or dumbbell

name: Cable Glute Kickback
instructions: Attach ankle cuff to low cable
Stand facing machine, slight forward lean
Kick leg back and up, squeezing glute
Return with control
Keep core braced, minimal hip rotation

name: Burpee
instructions: Start standing, drop hands to floor
Jump feet back to push-up position
Perform one push-up (optional)
Jump feet to hands
Explosively jump up with arms overhead

name: Kettlebell Swing
instructions: Hinge at hips, hike kettlebell back
Drive hips forward explosively
Bell swings to shoulder height
Let bell fall back, hinge again
Power comes from hips, not arms

name: Dumbbell Clean and Press
instructions: Start with dumbbells at sides
Clean to shoulders in one explosive motion
Press overhead to full extension
Lower dumbbells back to shoulders
Return to starting position

name: Farmer's Walk
instructions: Pick up heavy dumbbells or kettlebells
Walk forward with upright posture
Take short, controlled steps
Keep shoulders back and down
Great for grip strength and conditioning

name: Hip Flexor Stretch
instructions: Kneel on one knee, other foot forward
Shift weight forward until stretch in front hip
Keep torso upright
Hold 30-60 seconds
Switch sides

name: Standing Hamstring Stretch
instructions: Stand and hinge forward at hips
Reach toward toes, keeping back flat
Hold when you feel hamstring tension
Breathe and relax deeper into stretch
Hold 30-60 seconds

name: Doorway Chest Stretch
instructions: Stand in doorway, arms at 90°
Step forward until stretch in chest
Hold 30-60 seconds
Vary arm height to target different fibers
Great counter to desk posture

name: Cat-Cow Stretch
instructions: Start on hands and knees
Arch back up (cat), tuck chin
Then dip belly down (cow), lift head
Move slowly and breathe
Great warm-up for spine mobility

name: Pigeon Pose
instructions: From plank, bring one knee toward same-side wrist
Extend other leg straight back
Lower hips toward floor
Hold 1-2 minutes per side
Best post-workout glute/hip stretch

name: T-Bar Row
instructions: Load one end of a barbell into a corner or use a T-bar row machine
Straddle the bar and grip the handle with both hands
Hinge at hips until torso is 45 degrees
Pull bar toward lower chest squeezing shoulder blades together
Lower with control and repeat

name: Back Extension
instructions: Position yourself on hyperextension bench, hips at pad edge
Cross arms over chest or hold behind head
Lower torso toward floor maintaining neutral spine
Raise back up until body is in a straight line
Do not hyperextend beyond neutral at the top

name: Good Morning
instructions: Place barbell across upper back as with squat
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart
Hinge at hips pushing them backward
Lower torso until parallel to floor keeping back straight
Drive hips forward to return to standing

name: Dumbbell Pullover
instructions: Lie perpendicular on bench, upper back supported
Hold one dumbbell with both hands above chest
Lower dumbbell in arc behind head keeping slight elbow bend
Pull back to starting position using lat muscles
Keep hips low throughout movement

name: Machine Chest Press
instructions: Adjust seat so handles align with mid-chest
Grip handles with palms facing down
Press forward until arms are nearly extended
Return with control — do not let weight stack touch
Keep back flat against pad throughout

name: Cable Crossover
instructions: Set pulleys to high position on cable crossover machine
Grab handles and step forward with one foot
With slight elbow bend, bring hands together in front of chest
Squeeze chest at peak contraction
Slowly return to start position

name: Upright Row
instructions: Stand holding barbell with overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart
Pull bar straight up along body to chin height
Lead with elbows — they should rise above wrists
Pause briefly at top
Lower with control to starting position

name: Barbell Shrug
instructions: Stand holding barbell in front of thighs with overhand grip
Shrug shoulders straight up toward ears
Hold peak contraction for 1-2 seconds
Lower shoulders fully before next rep
Do not roll shoulders — purely vertical movement

name: Hack Squat
instructions: Position feet shoulder-width on foot plate, toes slightly out
Unlock safety handles and lower by bending knees
Squat until thighs are at least parallel to platform
Drive through heels to push back up
Lock safety handles when complete

name: Sumo Squat
instructions: Stand with feet wider than shoulder-width, toes pointing out 45 degrees
Hold one dumbbell with both hands vertically
Squat down keeping chest tall and knees tracking over toes
Drive through heels to return to standing
Squeeze glutes at the top

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
instructions: Stand 2-3 feet in front of a bench or box
Place rear foot on bench, laces down
Lower into a lunge keeping front shin vertical
Descend until rear knee nearly touches floor
Drive through front heel to stand

name: Box Jump
instructions: Stand facing a sturdy plyo box, feet hip-width apart
Dip into a quarter squat, swinging arms back
Explode up driving arms forward, landing softly on box
Land with both feet simultaneously, absorbing impact with bent knees
Step down — do not jump down to protect joints

name: Step-Up
instructions: Stand in front of a bench or sturdy step holding dumbbells
Step one foot onto the bench
Drive through that heel to lift body up
Bring trailing foot up to stand on bench
Step back down and repeat on same leg or alternate

name: Sumo Deadlift
instructions: Stand with feet much wider than shoulder-width, toes pointed out
Grip bar inside stance with narrow grip
Lower hips, keep chest tall and spine neutral
Drive floor away with legs while pulling bar into body
Lock out hips and knees simultaneously at top

name: Incline Dumbbell Curl
instructions: Sit on incline bench set to 45-60 degrees
Let arms hang straight down holding dumbbells
Curl both dumbbells up keeping elbows stationary
Squeeze biceps at peak
Lower slowly for full stretch — the stretch is key with this variation

name: Concentration Curl
instructions: Sit on bench, lean forward and brace elbow on inner thigh
Hold dumbbell with palm facing up
Curl dumbbell up while keeping upper arm stationary
Squeeze bicep hard at peak
Lower slowly to full arm extension

name: Reverse Curl
instructions: Hold barbell with overhand (pronated) grip, shoulder-width
Stand with arms fully extended
Curl bar upward keeping elbows at sides
Lower slowly — the eccentric is critical for forearm development
Do not swing torso

name: Close-Grip Bench Press
instructions: Lie on flat bench, grip bar with hands 10-12 inches apart
Unrack bar and hold above chest with locked arms
Lower bar to lower chest keeping elbows tucked close to body
Press back to full extension
Keep wrists straight throughout

name: Cable Overhead Tricep Extension
instructions: Set cable pulley to low position, attach rope handle
Stand facing away from machine, grab rope overhead
Lean slightly forward with elbows by ears
Extend arms overhead straightening elbows
Return slowly maintaining upper arm position

name: Diamond Push-Up
instructions: Get into push-up position
Place hands directly under chest forming a diamond shape with thumbs and index fingers
Lower chest toward hands keeping elbows close to body
Push back up to starting position
Keep core rigid throughout

name: Bicycle Crunch
instructions: Lie on back with hands lightly behind head
Lift shoulder blades off floor and raise knees to 45 degrees
Bring right elbow toward left knee while extending right leg
Alternate sides in a pedaling motion
Keep lower back pressed to floor throughout

name: Cable Crunch
instructions: Kneel below a high pulley cable with rope attachment
Hold rope at sides of head, elbows wide
Crunch down curling spine — focus on bringing ribs to hips
Hold contraction at bottom for 1-2 seconds
Return to upright position with control

name: Mountain Climber
instructions: Start in high push-up position with hands under shoulders
Drive one knee toward chest while keeping other leg extended
Quickly switch legs in a running motion
Keep hips level — do not bounce up and down
Maintain braced core throughout

name: Dead Bug
instructions: Lie on back with arms extended toward ceiling, knees at 90 degrees
Press lower back firmly into floor — maintain throughout
Lower right arm and left leg simultaneously toward floor
Return to start before lowering left arm and right leg
Exhale on the lowering phase

name: Battle Rope Waves
instructions: Stand in athletic stance facing anchor point, knees slightly bent
Hold one end of each rope with neutral grip
Alternately raise and lower each arm creating waves
Keep core braced and hips low
Work in 20-30 second intervals with 10-20 seconds rest

name: Stair Climber
instructions: Step onto machine and hold handrails lightly
Set pace — 60-80 steps per minute is moderate intensity
Push down through entire foot, not just toes
Keep back straight and avoid leaning on handrails
Vary speed for interval training

name: Jump Squat
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart
Squat down to parallel, loading through heels
Explode upward jumping as high as possible
Land softly with knees bent absorbing impact
Immediately transition into next squat

name: Single-Leg Glute Bridge
instructions: Lie on back with knees bent, one foot flat on floor
Extend other leg straight parallel to bent thigh
Drive through grounded heel lifting hips toward ceiling
Squeeze glute hard at the top
Lower hips with control without touching floor between reps

name: Donkey Kick
instructions: Start on all fours with hands under shoulders and knees under hips
Keep one knee bent at 90 degrees and kick it toward ceiling
Squeeze glute at peak, foot flexed
Lower leg without letting knee touch floor
Complete all reps on one side before switching

name: Banded Clamshell
instructions: Place resistance band just above knees
Lie on side with hips stacked, knees bent at 45 degrees
Keep feet together and rotate top knee toward ceiling
Open as far as possible without rotating pelvis
Lower with control

name: Standing Quad Stretch
instructions: Stand on one leg, holding the other ankle behind you
Pull ankle toward glutes gently
Keep knees together and stand tall
Hold 20-30 seconds
Switch legs

name: Cross-Body Shoulder Stretch
instructions: Bring one arm across body at shoulder height
Hook opposite arm under and press arm toward chest
Hold 20-30 seconds feeling stretch in rear shoulder
Relax and switch sides

name: Overhead Tricep Stretch
instructions: Raise one arm overhead and bend elbow so hand reaches behind head
Use opposite hand to gently push elbow further down
Hold 20-30 seconds
Switch arms

name: Cobra Stretch
instructions: Lie face down with palms flat beside shoulders
Press up straightening arms while hips remain on floor
Lift chest as high as comfortable
Hold 20-30 seconds
Lower slowly

name: Lateral Side Stretch
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart
Raise one arm overhead and lean to opposite side
Reach as far as comfortable feeling stretch along side body
Hold 20-30 seconds
Switch sides

name: Thread the Needle
instructions: Start on all fours with hands under shoulders
Slide one arm under torso along floor rotating thoracic spine
Let shoulder and ear rest on ground
Hold 20-30 seconds feeling rotation in upper back
Return and repeat on other side

name: Cable Bicep Curl
instructions: Set cable pulley to low position, attach straight bar or EZ bar
Stand facing machine, grip bar underhand shoulder-width
Curl bar up to shoulder height keeping elbows fixed
Squeeze at peak for 1 second
Lower slowly — cable maintains tension throughout

name: Machine Shoulder Press
instructions: Adjust seat so handles are at shoulder height
Grip handles with palms facing forward
Press up until arms are nearly fully extended
Lower handles back to shoulder height with control
Keep core braced and avoid leaning back

name: Dumbbell Shrug
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at sides with neutral grip
Shrug shoulders straight up toward ears
Hold peak contraction for 1-2 seconds
Lower fully before next rep
Keep arms straight throughout — no elbow bend

name: Pallof Press
instructions: Set cable to chest height and stand perpendicular to machine
Hold handle at chest with both hands
Press handle straight out in front maintaining neutral spine
Resist the rotational pull of the cable — this is the anti-rotation challenge
Return to chest and repeat

name: Hip Abduction Machine
instructions: Sit with pads against outer thighs
Sit upright with back against pad
Push knees outward against pad resistance
Hold briefly at maximum separation
Return slowly — do not slam weight stack

name: Hip Adduction Machine
instructions: Sit with pads against inner thighs
Start with legs apart in the machine's range
Squeeze knees together against resistance
Hold peak contraction for 1 second
Open legs slowly back to start
